frameworks.name: functional connectomics
frameworks.one_line: individual differences indexed by whole-brain functional connectivity profiles

frameworks.name: transdiagnostic psychiatric dimensions
frameworks.one_line: symptom spectra cross diagnostic boundaries and map onto network function

frameworks.name: categorical psychiatric nosology
frameworks.one_line: discrete diagnostic categories assumed to reflect distinct neurobiology

core_question: Do functional connectome disruptions track transdiagnostic affective and psychotic symptom dimensions rather than discrete diagnoses?
key_assumptions: psychiatric diagnoses lack sharp neurobiological boundaries
behavioral variability maps onto functional connectome variation
illness severity scales graded network disruption
manipulations: diagnostic group (primary psychotic, affective without psychosis, healthy)
presence vs absence of psychotic symptoms
key_findings: shared connectomic fingerprints disrupted across distinct pathologies
frontoparietal network connectivity disrupted in graded fashion with illness
default network integrity preferentially disrupted in psychotic illness only
interpretations: transdiagnostic symptom spectra correspond to detectable network-function patterns
some network disruptions are general while others are psychosis-specific
